questions.text: Accelerating electromagnetic charges radiate electromagnetic waves which propagate at the
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is a redundant phrase, as electromagnetic waves propagate at the speed of light.
Frequency is a measure of the number of oscillations per second, not a speed.

Accelerating electromagnetic charges, such as electrons, radiate electromagnetic waves, which are a combination of electric and magnetic fields. These waves propagate through the electromagnetic field at a constant speed, which is:c = 299,792,458 meters per second (m/s)This speed is known as the speed of light, and it is a fundamental constant of the universe
While electromagnetic waves do have a frequency, this is not the speed at which they propagate.

options.explanation: 10 kHz: While 10 kHz is a vibration, it's lower than 0.1 MHz.

Explanation:Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz). Higher frequency means more cycles per second.1 kHz = 1000 Hz, 1 MHz = 1,000,000 Hz.0.1 MHz = 100,000 Hz, which is the highest frequency among the given options.10 kHz is 10,000 Hz. 1000Hz is 1 kHz.
1 kHz: 1 kHz is significantly lower than 0.1 MHz
1000Hz: 1000hz is equal to 1khz, so this answer is also significantly lower than 0.1Mhz

questions.text: An object moving faster than the speed of sound. This object is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When an object is moving faster than the speed of sound in a medium, it is considered supersonic. The speed of sound varies depending on the medium, but in air at sea level, it is approximately 343 meters per second. If an object exceeds this speed, it is said to be supersonic.
Hypersonic refers to speeds significantly higher than the speed of sound.
Ultrasonic refers to frequencies or waves that are beyond the range of human hearing, typically above 20,000 Hertz.
Infrasonic refers to frequencies or waves that are below the range of human hearing, typically below 20 Hertz.

questions.explanation: When an object is moving faster than the speed of sound in a medium, it is considered supersonic. The speed of sound varies depending on the medium, but in the air at sea level, it is approximately 343 meters per second. If an object exceeds this speed, it is said to be supersonic. So option B is correct.
